competency_groups.title: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.description: Understanding the photoelectric effect and wave-particle duality.
competencies: Explain the experimental evidence for the particle nature of light through the photoelectric effect.
Describe de Broglie's hypothesis regarding the wave nature of matter particles.
Analyze the relationship between photon energy, frequency, and work function.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms
competency_groups.description: Exploring atomic structure, models, and spectral series.
competencies: Evaluate Rutherford's alpha-particle scattering experiment and its implications for nuclear model.
Apply Bohr's postulates to explain the stability of atoms and hydrogen spectrum.
Identify different spectral series (Lyman, Balmer, Paschen) derived from electron transitions.

competency_groups.title: Nuclei
competency_groups.description: Investigating nuclear properties, binding energy, and reactions.
competencies: Calculate mass defect and binding energy per nucleon to explain nuclear stability.
Differentiate between nuclear fission and fusion processes with real-world examples.
Analyze the characteristics of radioactive decay laws and half-life.

competency_groups.title: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
competency_groups.description: Studying semiconductor physics and electronic device applications.
competencies: Distinguish between intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors based on doping types.
Explain the working principles of p-n junction diodes in rectification circuits.
Design simple logic gate combinations using basic transistor switches.
